title: दश महाविद्या स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री दश महाविद्या स्तोत्रम् ॥

ॐ श्रीं ह्रीं क्लीं देव्यै नमः।

महाकाली महालक्ष्मी महासरस्वती तथा।
दश विद्या महाविद्या सिद्धविद्याः प्रकीर्तिताः॥१॥

काली तारा महाविद्या षोडशी भुवनेश्वरी।
भैरवी छिन्नमस्ता च विद्या धूमावती तथा॥२॥

बगला सिद्धविद्या च मातंगी कमलात्मिका।
एता दश महाविद्याः सिद्धविद्याः प्रकीर्तिताः॥३॥

॥ काली ध्यानम् ॥
क्रीं क्रीं क्रीं कालिकायै नमः।
करालवदनां घोरां मुक्तकेशीं चतुर्भुजाम्।
कालिकां दक्षिणां दिव्यां मुण्डमालाविभूषिताम्॥४॥

॥ तारा ध्यानम् ॥
ॐ ह्रीं स्त्रीं हूं फट् तारायै नमः।
प्रत्यालीढपदां घोरां मुण्डमालाविभूषिताम्।
खड्गकर्त्रिसमायुक्तां तारां देवीं नमाम्यहम्॥५॥

॥ षोडशी ध्यानम् ॥
ऐं क्लीं सौः श्रीं षोडश्यै नमः।
बालार्कमण्डलाभासां चतुर्बाहुसमन्विताम्।
पाशाङ्कुशधनुर्बाणधरां त्रिपुरसुन्दरीम्॥६॥

॥ भुवनेश्वरी ध्यानम् ॥
ह्रीं भुवनेश्वर्यै नमः।
उद्यद्भानुसहस्राभां त्रिनेत्रां रत्नमौलिनीम्।
पाशाङ्कुशवराभीतिधरां भुवनेश्वरीम्॥७॥

॥ भैरवी ध्यानम् ॥
ह्रीं भैरव्यै नमः।
सौवर्णवर्णसम्पन्नां त्रिनेत्रां वरदायिनीम्।
रक्तवस्त्रपरीधानां भैरवीं प्रणमाम्यहम्॥८॥

॥ छिन्नमस्ता ध्यानम् ॥
श्रीं ह्रीं क्लीं ऐं वज्रवैरोचनीये ह्रीं ह्रीं फट् स्वाहा।
प्रचण्डचण्डिकां देवीं छिन्नमस्तां नमाम्यहम्।
स्वशिरश्छेदिनीं देवीं रक्तधारास्वयम्पिबाम्॥९॥

॥ धूमावती ध्यानम् ॥
धूं धूं धूमावत्यै नमः।
विवर्णा चञ्चला रुष्टा दीर्घा च मलिनाम्बरा।
विमुक्तकुन्तलां शुष्कां धूमावतीं नमाम्यहम्॥१०॥

॥ बगलामुखी ध्यानम् ॥
ॐ ह्लीं बगलामुख्यै नमः।
पीताम्बरपरीधानां पीताभरणभूषिताम्।
गदामुसलहस्तां च बगलां प्रणमाम्यहम्॥११॥

॥ मातंगी ध्यानम् ॥
ॐ ह्रीं क्लीं हूं मातंग्यै नमः।
श्यामां शुकधरां देवीं वीणावादनतत्पराम्।
मातंगीं प्रणतोऽस्म्यहं सर्वसिद्धिप्रदायिनीम्॥१२॥

॥ कमला ध्यानम् ॥
ॐ ऐं ह्रीं श्रीं क्लीं कमलायै नमः।
काञ्चनाभां गजद्वन्द्वसिञ्चिताम्बुविराजिताम्।
पद्मासनस्थिताम्भोजकरां कमलां नमाम्यहम्॥१३॥

॥ फलश्रुतिः ॥
दश विद्या महाविद्या सर्वसिद्धिविधायिनीः।
यः पठेत्प्रातरुत्थाय स सर्वज्ञो भवेन्नरः॥१४॥

ऐश्वर्यमतुलं लब्ध्वा सर्वान्कामानवाप्नुयात्।
शत्रुनाशं भवेत्तस्य दश विद्या प्रसादतः॥१५॥

॥ इति श्री दश महाविद्या स्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Dasha Mahavidya Stotram ||

Om Shrim Hrim Klim Devyai Namah.

Mahakali Mahalakshmi Mahasarasvati Tatha.
Dasha Vidya Mahavidya Siddha-vidyah Prakirtitah.||1||

Kali Tara Mahavidya Shodashi Bhuvaneshwari.
Bhairavi Chhinnamasta Cha Vidya Dhumavati Tatha.||2||

Bagala Siddha-vidya Cha Matangi Kamalatmika.
Eta Dasha Mahavidyah Siddha-vidyah Prakirtitah.||3||

|| Kali Dhyanam ||
Krim Krim Krim Kalikayai Namah.
Karala-vadanam Ghoram Mukta-keshim Chaturbhujam.
Kalikam Dakshinam Divyam Munda-mala-vibhushitam.||4||

|| Tara Dhyanam ||
Om Hrim Strim Hum Phat Tarayai Namah.
Pratyalidha-padam Ghoram Munda-mala-vibhushitam.
Khadga-kartri-samayuktam Taram Devim Namamyaham.||5||

|| Shodashi Dhyanam ||
Aim Klim Sauh Shrim Shodashyai Namah.
Balarka-mandala-bhasam Chatur-bahu-samanvitam.
Pasha-ankusha-dhanur-bana-dharam Tripurasundarim.||6||

|| Bhuvaneshwari Dhyanam ||
Hrim Bhuvaneshwaryai Namah.
Udyad-bhanu-sahasrabham Trinetram Ratna-maulinim.
Pasha-ankusha-vara-bhiti-dharam Bhuvaneshwarim.||7||

|| Bhairavi Dhyanam ||
Hrim Bhairavyai Namah.
Sauvarna-varna-sampannam Trinetram Varadayinim.
Rakta-vastra-paridhanam Bhairavim Pranamamyaham.||8||

|| Chhinnamasta Dhyanam ||
Shrim Hrim Klim Aim Vajra-vairochaniye Hrim Hrim Phat Svaha.
Prachanda-chandikam Devim Chhinnamastam Namamyaham.
Sva-shirash-chedinim Devim Rakta-dhara-svayam-pibam.||9||

|| Dhumavati Dhyanam ||
Dhum Dhum Dhumavatyai Namah.
Vivarna Chanchala Rushta Dirgha Cha Malinambara.
Vimukta-kuntalam Shushkam Dhumavatim Namamyaham.||10||

|| Bagalamukhi Dhyanam ||
Om Hlim Bagalamukhyai Namah.
Pitambara-paridhanam Pitabharana-bhushitam.
Gada-musala-hastam Cha Bagalam Pranamamyaham.||11||

|| Matangi Dhyanam ||
Om Hrim Klim Hum Matangyai Namah.
Shyamam Shuka-dharam Devim Vina-vadana-tatparam.
Matangim Pranato'smyaham Sarva-siddhi-pradayinim.||12||

|| Kamala Dhyanam ||
Om Aim Hrim Shrim Klim Kamalayai Namah.
Kanchanabham Gaja-dvandva-sinchitambu-virajitam.
Padmasana-sthitambhoja-karam Kamalam Namamyaham.||13||

|| Phalashrutih ||
Dasha Vidya Mahavidya Sarva-siddhi-vidhayinih.
Yah Pathet Pratar Utthaya Sa Sarvajno Bhaven Narah.||14||

Aishvaryam Atulam Labdhva Sarvan Kaman Avapnuyat.
Shatru-nasham Bhavet Tasya Dasha Vidya Prasadatah.||15||

|| Iti Shri Dasha Mahavidya Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
काली, तारा, षोडशी (त्रिपुरसुन्दरी), भुवनेश्वरी, भैरवी, छिन्नमस्ता, धूमावती, बगलामुखी, मातंगी और कमला — ये दश महाविद्याएँ सिद्ध विद्याएँ कही गई हैं।

काली — विकराल मुखवाली, मुक्त केशी, चतुर्भुजा, मुण्डमाला से विभूषित दक्षिणा काली।

तारा — प्रत्यालीढ पादवाली, मुण्डमालाधारिणी, खड्ग-कर्तरी धारिणी तारा देवी।

षोडशी — बालसूर्य की कान्ति वाली, पाश, अंकुश, धनुष, बाण धारिणी त्रिपुरसुन्दरी।

भुवनेश्वरी — सहस्र सूर्यों की प्रभा वाली, त्रिनेत्रा, पाश-अंकुश-वर-अभय धारिणी।

भैरवी — स्वर्ण वर्ण, त्रिनेत्रा, वरदायिनी, रक्तवस्त्र धारिणी।

छिन्नमस्ता — स्वयं अपना शीश काटने वाली, रक्तधारा स्वयं पीने वाली।

धूमावती — विवर्ण, चंचला, मलिन वस्त्र वाली, शुष्क रूप।

बगलामुखी — पीताम्बर धारिणी, पीत आभरणों से भूषित, गदा-मुसल धारिणी।

मातंगी — श्यामवर्णा, शुकधारिणी, वीणावादन में तत्पर, सर्वसिद्धि प्रदायिनी।

कमला — स्वर्णाभा, गजयुगल से अभिषिक्त, पद्मासन पर स्थित, कमलधारिणी।

जो प्रातः इस स्तोत्र को पढ़ता है, वह सर्वज्ञ होता है, अतुल ऐश्वर्य प्राप्त करता है और शत्रुनाश होता है।
benefits: Invokes all ten Mahavidya forms of the Supreme Goddess
Invokes complete spiritual knowledge and sarvajnatva (omniscience)
Invokes the blessing of incomparable wealth, power, and prosperity
Traditionally recited for removal of all enemies through the grace of ten mahavidyas
Provides mastery over all tantric and spiritual practices
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of doshas and grants supreme protection
description: Among the most cherished prayers to Goddess Durga, Dasha Mahavidya Stotram (दश महाविद्या स्तोत्रम्) stands as a devotional hymn of praise. Attributed to Tantra Shastra, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Devotees recite this prayer to provide mastery over all tantric and spiritual practices and invoke all ten Mahavidya forms of the Supreme Goddess. It is also said to bestow incomparable wealth, power, and prosperity. For best results, devotees chant this on Tuesday (Mangalvar), Saturday (Shanivar), and Friday (Shukravar) and during Navratri, Kali Puja, and Diwali. Comprising 15 verses, it can be completed in approximately 12 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Dasha Mahavidya Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Dasha Mahavidya Stotram in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Prayer to all 10 Mahavidyas - Kali, Tara, Shodashi, Bhuvaneshwari and more.
relatedSearches: Kali Tara Tripura Sundari Bhuvaneshwari mantras

seoFaqs.question: Who are the ten Mahavidyas invoked in this stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: The Dasha Mahavidya Stotram invokes the ten principal Tantric forms of the Devi in their canonical order: Kali, Tara, Tripura Sundari (also called Shodashi or Sri Vidya), Bhuvaneshwari, Bhairavi, Chinnamasta, Dhumavati, Bagalamukhi, Matangi and Kamala. This list is reproduced consistently across Shakta sources including Rudraksha Ratna's Mahavidya compendium and the Drikpanchang mantra collection. Each of the ten represents a different aspect of cosmic Shakti, ranging from time and dissolution (Kali) to abundance and material grace (Kamala). The stotram dedicates a verse to each Mahavidya, naming her distinguishing iconography, weapon and the specific siddhi she grants, before concluding with a unifying salutation to all ten as a single Mahavidya Shakti.

seoFaqs.question: Where does the Dasha Mahavidya tradition originate scripturally?
seoFaqs.answer: The Dasha Mahavidya schema is primarily a Tantric formulation rather than a Vedic one, with its earliest detailed treatment found in texts such as the Mahabhagavata Purana, the Brihaddharma Purana and various Shakta Tantras including the Todala Tantra and Mundamala Tantra. The standard origin myth ties the ten forms to the Daksha Yajna episode, where Sati manifests the ten Mahavidyas around Shiva to demonstrate her cosmic identity before departing for her father's sacrifice. This narrative is summarised on Rudraksha Ratna and Hindupedia. The stotram circulating today is a curated compilation that draws verses from these Tantric sources rather than a single chapter from one text, which is why different printed editions occasionally vary in verse order or epithets.

seoFaqs.question: Why are the Mahavidyas called 'Vidyas' rather than simply Devis?
seoFaqs.answer: In Tantric usage, a Vidya is a feminine wisdom-form whose mantra is itself the deity. Calling the ten 'Mahavidyas' (great wisdoms) rather than only Devis emphasises that each is approached primarily through her mantra, yantra and sadhana rather than through purely iconic worship. As Astromantra and Stotram.co.in summarise, of the hundreds of Tantric practices the worship of these ten major Devis is collectively called the Dasha Mahavidya because each grants a specific transcendental knowledge. The stotram therefore functions both as a praise-hymn and as a mnemonic of the ten core Vidyas, helping the sadhaka hold their sequence, attributes and bija sounds in continuous remembrance during nyasa and dhyana.

seoFaqs.question: Are the ten Mahavidyas worshipped together or individually?
seoFaqs.answer: Both modes exist in living tradition. Most initiated sadhakas receive a single Mahavidya as their ishta (Kali, Tara and Tripura Sundari being the most common in Bengal, Assam and South India respectively) and pursue her sadhana intensively. The Dasha Mahavidya Stotram, however, is recited as a collective hymn, particularly during Navratri, on Mahavidya Jayantis, and in the daily worship at major Shakta peethas where all ten are installed. Reciting the full stotram is considered safe for non-initiates because it offers respectful praise to all forms without invoking the more austere bija mantras (such as those of Dhumavati or Bagalamukhi) that traditionally require diksha. Pandits therefore recommend the stotram as the entry point to Mahavidya worship for householders.

seoFaqs.question: Which Mahavidyas are considered fierce and which are gentle?
seoFaqs.answer: Tantric texts classify the ten into ugra (fierce) and saumya (gentle) groups, though the boundary is fluid. Kali, Tara, Chinnamasta, Dhumavati, Bagalamukhi and Bhairavi are usually counted among the ugra forms, associated with cremation grounds, paralysing speech, smoke, and severed-head imagery. Tripura Sundari, Bhuvaneshwari, Matangi and Kamala are generally treated as saumya, associated with beauty, queenship of the world, classical arts and lotus-borne abundance. Sources such as Rudraksha Ratna and Hindupedia note that this classification governs the kind of upasana prescribed: the fierce forms typically require strict diksha and are not casually worshipped, while the saumya forms are openly chanted in households. The stotram preserves this distinction by adapting tone and adjective for each verse.

seoFaqs.question: Is it safe for a beginner to recite the Dasha Mahavidya Stotram at home?
seoFaqs.answer: Yes, the stotram form, which only praises the ten Mahavidyas without activating their Tantric bija mantras, is considered appropriate for householders and is regularly published in mainstream collections such as those by Astromantra, Stotram.co.in and TheDivineIndia. Traditional teachers nonetheless recommend a few precautions: bathe and wear clean clothes, sit facing east or north, light a ghee lamp, and avoid reciting the stotram casually after meat or alcohol. Beginners are advised to start with a sankalpa to recite once daily for 41 days, and to refrain from undertaking the dedicated bija japa of fierce Mahavidyas such as Dhumavati or Bagalamukhi without initiation from a competent guru. Treated as a praise-hymn, the stotram itself carries no such restrictions.
